events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario involves fall protection equipment with frayed/burned webbing. This is clearly defective and must be taken out of service immediately. Frayed or burned webbing compromises the integrity of the harness/lanyard and cannot be used.

Key points:
- Frayed/burned webbing on fall protection equipment
- This is a clear defect - the equipment must be removed from service
- OSHA 1910.140(c)(18) states that personal fall protection equipment must be inspected before use and removed from service if any defects are found
- OSHA 1926 Subpart M requires proper fall protection equipment in good condition

Decision: fail
Risk: critical (fall protection failure could lead to death)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant, degraded, worn, failed
Percent complete: estimate around 85% since it's rough-complete
Value: 0 (no numeric measurement specifically requested, but the condition is defective)
Confidence: high - frayed/burned webbing is clearly defective

Let me write the JSON.

  "findings": [
    "Frayed and/or burned webbing observed on fall protection equipment — harness or lanyard webbing is compromised and cannot support rated fall loads",
    "Burned or heat-damaged webbing indicates loss of tensile strength and potential hidden fiber damage not visible on the surface",
    "Equipment is installed/available for use in a defective state — must be immediately removed from service"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately remove the damaged harness/lanyard from service and tag it as defective",
    "Inspect all other fall protection components on site for similar damage",
    "Replace with new, manufacturer-certified equipment before any work at height resumes",
    "Document the defect and retain for inspection records"
  ],
  "rationale": "Frayed or burned webbing on a personal fall arrest system is an automatic out-of-service condition. OSHA requires that personal fall protection equipment be inspected before each use and removed from service if any wear, damage, or degradation is found. Burned or frayed webbing severely reduces the breaking strength of the strap and can fail catastrophically under fall arrest loading. This is a critical life-safety hazard.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.140(c)(18) — equipment must be inspected before use and removed from service if defective",
    "OSHA 1910.140(d) — equipment inspection requirements",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart M 1926.502(d)(21) — personal fall arrest systems shall be inspected prior to each use for wear, damage, and other deterioration, and defective components shall be removed from service",
    "ANSI Z359.1 — fall protection equipment inspection and retirement criteria"
  ]
}
